page: Vultr vx1-m-32c-256gb is a memory-optimized server belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features 32 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC-Turin processor with an x86_64 architecture, running at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The CPU allocation is shared, utilizing 32 physical cores with 1 thread per core. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Local storage is limited to a 1 GB network-attached volume, and no GPU accelerators are present.

In benchmark evaluations, this server demonstrates strong memory performance, highlighted by a top-tier rating in the PassMark memory score and large-block memory write bandwidth. Single-core processing is robust, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and single-threaded gzip compression scoring in the top tiers. Multi-core CPU performance under stress-ng is average, reflecting the shared CPU allocation model. Cryptographic and vector instruction capabilities are supported via AVX-512 and SHA-NI instruction sets, yielding strong results in PassMark CPU extended instructions and encryption tests.

The shared CPU allocation presents a performance tradeoff for sustained multi-core workloads, though it offers a cost-effective entry point for memory-intensive applications that do not require dedicated CPU cores. Given the 256.0 GB memory capacity and strong database operation benchmarks, this server is well-suited for memory-cached databases, Redis deployments, static web serving, and memory-heavy data processing tasks.
description: Vultr vx1-m-32c-256gb is a memory-optimized server from the VX1 Cloud Compute family, featuring 32 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz and 256.0 GB of RAM. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this configuration provides a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It relies on a 1 GB network-attached storage volume and does not include a GPU. Benchmarks show top-tier memory write bandwidth and PassMark memory scores, alongside strong single-core CPU performance, though multi-core performance is average due to the shared CPU model. This server is highly effective for memory-intensive workloads such as Redis caching, database operations, and static web serving, offering a cost-efficient profile for high-memory requirements without the expense of dedicated CPU cores.
og_description: Vultr vx1-m-32c-256gb is a memory-optimized server featuring 32 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and network storage, delivering strong memory bandwidth for database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vx1-m-32c-256gb is a memory-optimized server with 32 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and strong memory bandwidth for database workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity memory-optimized server featuring thirty-two shared cores and top-tier memory bandwidth for caching and database workloads.

256.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
Top-tier memory write bandwidth and PassMark memory scores
1 GB network-attached local storage with no GPU acceleration
Optimized for Redis deployments, database operations, and static web serving
